reviews.review_description:
Me and my friend played this game the day or the day after the games release, it is and was amazing. Today we log on to see the new backrooms update as there aren't many good backrooms games, all of our progress was reset, we kept our mana coins and shards, + levels to show that we played.We log on and see a intro for a new player, confused we continued playing and realized that ALL of our spells were gone. Dont grind this game or spend money unless your okay with it getting unfairly taken away. 


PS: My friend spent money and he lost it.

developer_response.body:
You were given a refund of exactly:
- 600 Shards
- 16,250 Gold

This was your full refund for all the spells & perks you owned, now why we did this was because as you know Wizherd is in Early Access which means it's constantly changing & improving which our progression is one of these things that tend to sometimes change, mostly for the better.

You have not lost anything in your account except from spells you can simply just re-purchase again identically as if they were never removed, the reason it was done was to prevent/remove any unfair progression other players may have previously had happen to them. Simply buy the same spells you previously owned with your refunded currency and it would be like nothing ever happened.

I am honestly unsure if I'll ever end up re-downloading and if I do, please fix this god dam problem.

- 18,750 Gold

You are not restarting back at the beginning at all, you just received an automatic refund for all of your in-game spells which you can simply either buy new spells or the exact same ones you previously owned before as if nothing  changed

reviews.review_description:
I don’t usually write reviews, but Wizherd actually surprised me. The spell casting feels really natural and fun, like you’re actually controlling the magic instead of just clicking buttons. The combat is fast and can get pretty intense, especially when the enemies start getting tougher the further you go.

I really like how much freedom the game gives you. You can mix different spells, perks, and rings to match how you like to play, which keeps things from getting boring. The customization is also nice because you can change how your wizard looks while you play.

Playing with friends is probably my favorite part. It gets chaotic in the best way, and boss fights feel way more exciting with a team. Solo is still fun too if you want more of a challenge.

Overall, you can tell a lot of effort was put into the game and it keeps improving. If you like VR games or anything with magic and dungeon crawling, I definitely recommend trying Wizherd.

developer_response.body:
Glad to hear how much you've been enjoying Wizherd as you have likely seen, with each update we release the game just gets better and better.

We're proud to hear we're the best "gtag fan game" thank you lol
- HazBeann

I was super pumped to hear that Jman made another game. Wizherd is worth the time. The creatures and monsters are super fun to destroy. The creativity that went into the game and spells are crazy fun. Although to purchase them they are a little too grindy. I really love the lighting spells especially the lightning strand. The realms are super fun to explore. Me and my brother spend most of our time playing this game. We help newer players get the rewards for spells. Plus the newest storyline is insanely good I love the changes but I was really disappointed when we had the revamp but buying spells is so much easier with the refunds for what spells we bought before the revamp. Jman really great job with this game I really love it and I know others will. THIS GAME IS FREAKING AMAZING.

The spells are very well designed and the spell tree is evenly spread out so you actually have to play to get better stuff. The community is quite kind with only a bad/ mean person in a lobby for like every 50-100 servers or so. This game is a lot of fun when playing with friends or other people in public lobbies and playing solo is always good challenge to test your skills. I also just to point out that this game is free and very free-to-play friendly which is good because now games like UG and Animal Company are becoming very pay-to-win which makes a game aggravating ￼and boring to play so it’s nice that a game like this exists in the community. Just to rap this rating/ review up I really think the developers did a Grenoble and I hope they continue in a free to play friendly environment.

What was the point in working hard to level things up for it all to be rest and have to do it all again. I even had to do the tutorial again!!

developer_response.body:
You may mute them using the board which only takes a few moments to head on over to access just like most other vr games. You may also simply head on over to a private code simply by going to the number cruncher and joining one there where it's just you and up to 4 friends

- HazBeann

I honestly didn’t expect to love Wizherd this much. I’ve played a lot of VR games on Quest, especially multiplayer and fantasy games, but Wizherd feels genuinely special. The atmosphere, the magic system, the world design, the updates, the feeling of casting spells with your hands — it all feels incredibly fun and immersive. This is one of the few VR games where I completely lose track of time while playing.

The Castle Update especially made the game feel even more alive and magical. Exploring the environments, experimenting with spells, fighting enemies, and just existing in the world is already entertaining by itself. The visual style is also really charming. It has this cozy fantasy vibe while still feeling exciting and chaotic during battles.

What I love most is how creative the gameplay feels. A lot of VR games become repetitive quickly, but Wizherd constantly makes me want to try new combinations, new ways to fight, and new strategies with magic. The feeling of becoming a more powerful wizard over time is extremely satisfying.

The sound design is also underrated. The spell effects, impacts, ambience, and magical sounds make everything feel more immersive. Some moments genuinely feel like being inside a fantasy movie.

I also appreciate that the developers clearly care about the game and continue improving it. You can feel passion behind the project, and that matters a lot in VR. Many games on Quest feel unfinished or shallow, but Wizherd actually feels like a world with personality and potential.

One small thing I would improve is that some parts of the controls and menus can feel a little confusing at first, especially when learning how different abilities and systems work. After some time it becomes easier, but a smoother introduction or clearer tutorials would help new players a lot.

But overall, this game is honestly one of my favorite VR experiences. I rarely leave reviews, but Wizherd deserves it. Thank you for creating such a fun and magical game. I’m genuinely excited to see how it continues to evolve in the future.

Please keep supporting and expanding this game — it has huge potential and already feels like one of the most memorable fantasy VR games on Quest.

reviews.review_title: Got a serious problem with this ring bug
reviews.review_description:
I had two legendary rings and I was so happy…
